title: Secrets Handling Policy — Layer 1 (Detection) + Layer 2 (Prevention) Foundation + Active Leak Fixes
consumer_purpose: Provide the foundation for secrets detection and prevention by building the shared ENV_BLOCKLIST utility, fixing the three misnamed/absent env sanitizers, fixing the active OPENCODE_AUTH_CONTENT credential serialization leak, and extending the debug-packet redactor from key-name-only matching to full value scanning.
claim_atoms: ENV_BLOCKLIST constant enumerates all 31+ secret env vars from audit inventory
sanitizeEnv() filters any process.env copy through blocklist
sanitizedProcessEnv() in opencode-process.ts is no longer a no-op
createSidecarEnv() in server.ts strips blocklisted vars before passing to utilityProcess.fork()
cross-spawn-spawner.ts env() uses sanitizeEnv when extendEnv=true
OPENCODE_AUTH_CONTENT is in ENV_BLOCKLIST and stripped from all subprocesses except the workspace adapter that explicitly needs it
debug-packet.ts redactConfig gains value-scanning pass using http-recorder patterns
All existing tests continue to pass with no behavioral changes
Out-of-scope findings recorded: OAuth token encryption at rest (account.sql.ts, mcp/auth.ts) deferred to future layer
Out-of-scope finding recorded: enterprise storage.ts credential source refactoring deferred to future lane

root_causes.hypothesis: sanitizedProcessEnv() in opencode-process.ts:19 performs ZERO filtering despite the name suggesting sanitization — it's a straight Object.fromEntries on process.env
evidence: Replaces undefined-filtered entries directly, no blocklist check
Only caller: ripgrep.ts:148 passes all env vars to rg subprocess

root_causes.hypothesis: createSidecarEnv() in packages/desktop/src/main/server.ts:230 passes ALL process.env vars to the Electron utilityProcess sidecar
evidence: Sidecar inherits all API keys, tokens, server password, and storage credentials

root_causes.hypothesis: cross-spawn-spawner.ts env() passes all process.env when extendEnv=true
evidence: No filtering before spreading all env vars

root_causes.hypothesis: OPENCODE_AUTH_CONTENT serializes ALL auth credentials (OAuth tokens, API keys) into a single env var for a subprocess, leaking to all child processes
evidence: Read back in auth/index.ts:58-60 and account.ts:140-145
Any subprocess inheriting the env gets all credentials

root_causes.hypothesis: debug-packet.ts redactConfig only does key-name matching, missing values that contain secret patterns in non-key regions
evidence: isSensitiveKey only checks key name against SENSITIVE_PATTERNS
No scanning of actual values for bearer tokens, API keys, AWS keys
http-recorder/redaction.ts already has SecretFinding patterns — not reused

fixes.description: Create env-blocklist.ts shared utility — ENV_BLOCKLIST constant + sanitizeEnv() + isBlocklistedEnvVar()
fixes.before: No ENV_BLOCKLIST constant exists anywhere in codebase. sanitizedProcessEnv() is a no-op.
fixes.after:
New file packages/core/src/util/env-blocklist.ts exports:
- ENV_BLOCKLIST: string[] — full list of 31+ known secret env var names from cartographer inventory
- BLOCKLIST_PATTERNS: RegExp[] — suffix patterns (_KEY, _SECRET, _TOKEN, _PASSWORD, _CREDENTIAL, _TOKEN_ID, _SIGNING_SECRET, _APP_TOKEN, _CLIENT_ID, _CLIENT_SECRET)
- sanitizeEnv(source?: Record<string, string | undefined>, overrides?: Record<string, string>): Record<string, string> — copies source (default process.env), strips blocklisted vars, applies overrides
- isBlocklistedEnvVar(name: string): boolean — checks name against both exact list and suffix patterns

opencode-process.ts updated: sanitizedProcessEnv() delegates to sanitizeEnv()

tests_to_run: packages/opencode/test/ (all tests — ripgrep.ts is used throughout)
type_errors: None expected — function signatures match existing contract
sanitizeEnv return type matches what sanitizedProcessEnv returns

risks: Over-blocklisting could break subprocesses that need specific env vars. Mitigation: start with known secret names, keep BLOCKLIST_PATTERNS conservative (only suffix-based), add allowlist param

Some env vars like OPENCODE_MOTHERFUCKER_EXPLICITLY_ALLOW_THIS_ONE might have weird names that match suffix patterns. Mitigation: exact-match list takes priority over patterns, and sanitizeEnv supports allowlist override
validation: Unit test: sanitizeEnv(envWithSecret) returns env without secret
Unit test: isBlocklistedEnvVar returns true for known var names and suffix matches
Unit test: isBlocklistedEnvVar returns false for PLAIN_OLD_VAR
Integration: sanitizedProcessEnv() in ripgrep.ts continues to work (rg needs PATH, HOME, etc.)

fixes.description: Fix createSidecarEnv() in desktop/server.ts to use sanitizeEnv() filtering

tests_to_run: Manual: verify sidecar does not inherit OPENCODE_AUTH_CONTENT, OPENCODE_STORAGE_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, etc.
type_errors: None — import from @opencode-ai/core/util/env-blocklist is a valid path for desktop package

risks: Sidecar might legitimately need some env vars now blocked. Mitigation: start with conservative blocklist, monitor sidecar behavior
validation: After change, inspect env passed to utilityProcess.fork() — assert it does not contain blocklisted vars
Desktop health check still works (server password is injected separately via prepareSidecarEnv, not via createSidecarEnv)

fixes.description: Fix cross-spawn-spawner.ts env() to filter through sanitizeEnv when extendEnv=true

type_errors: None — sanitizeEnv returns Record<string, string>, spreads match

risks: Subprocesses that rely on inherited secret env vars will lose them. This is the INTENDED behavior — they shouldn't have them.
If opts.env contains blocklisted vars, they survive (overrides take precedence), which is correct for explicit env passing
validation: Verify opts.extendEnv && !opts.env behaves correctly (process.env filtered through blocklist)
Verify opts.extendEnv && opts.env behaves correctly (filtered env merged with explicit overrides)
Verify opts.extendEnv=false skips the whole block

fixes.description: Add OPENCODE_AUTH_CONTENT to ENV_BLOCKLIST — strips it from all subprocesses except the workspace adapter that explicitly sets it in opts.env
fixes.after: ENV_BLOCKLIST includes "OPENCODE_AUTH_CONTENT" — workspace.ts:570 sets it in explicit opts.env which takes priority over the blocklist, so the adapter still gets credentials
risks: If any code reads OPENCODE_AUTH_CONTENT from process.env without it being explicitly passed via opts.env, it will now read undefined. This is correct behavior — only the workspace adapter should have credentials.
auth/index.ts and account.ts both check process.env.OPENCODE_AUTH_CONTENT — they only need it when running within the adapter subprocess, where it's explicitly set in opts.env
validation: Verify workspace adapter process.env.OPENCODE_AUTH_CONTENT is set (explicit opts.env overrides blocklist)
Verify ripgrep subprocess does NOT have OPENCODE_AUTH_CONTENT
Verify sidecar subprocess does NOT have OPENCODE_AUTH_CONTENT
Run all provider tests that set OPENCODE_AUTH_CONTENT in test setup

fixes.description: Extend debug-packet.ts redactConfig to also scan values for secret patterns — reuse http-recorder's SECRET_PATTERNS
fixes.before: function redactConfig(config, depth) only checks key names via isSensitiveKey(). Values with secret patterns (bearer tokens, API keys) pass through unredacted.
fixes.after:
Add redactValues() pass after redactConfig() that scans ALL string values in the config object for secret patterns. Import SECRET_PATTERNS patterns from http-recorder/redaction.ts or define an equivalent local set.

New function:
- redactValues(obj, SECRET_PATTERNS) — deep-walks object, replaces any string value matching a secret pattern with "***REDACTED***"
- Called after redactConfig() in assembleDebugPacket()

affected_files: packages/opencode/src/debug/debug-packet.ts (add redactValues function, update assembleDebugPacket to call it)
tests_to_run: Manual: verify redacted config in debug output no longer contains bearer tokens or API keys
Manual: verify OPENCODE_AUTH_CONTENT-style values are redacted

risks: False positives: benign strings that look like API keys get redacted. Mitigation: use same conservative pattern set as http-recorder which has been battle-tested.
Performance: deep-walking large config objects could be slow. Mitigation: max depth parameter (already present in redactConfig), and config objects are typically < 500 entries.
validation: Unit test: string 'Bearer xxx123token456' is redacted
Unit test: benign string 'discussion-token approach works' is NOT redacted
Verify assembleDebugPacket output produces redactedConfig without secret values

out_of_scope_items.description: OAuth token encryption at rest in account.sql.ts and mcp/auth.ts — these store plaintext tokens in SQLite and JSON files. Requires platform-level encryption (Electron safeStorage pattern extension) which is Layer 5 (Integrity) work.
enterprise/src/core/storage.ts reads AWS S3/R2 credentials from process.env — should use SST Secret construct. This is an infrastructure + code refactor beyond Layers 1-2.
prepareSidecarEnv() in sidecar.ts:102 mutates process.env via Object.assign — this affects ALL subsequent code, not just the sidecar. Moving credential passing from env vars to IPC is future work.

rollback_plan: Revert each fix in reverse order: revert FIX-5 (debug-packet.ts), revert FIX-4 (env-blocklist.ts line addition), revert FIX-3 (cross-spawn-spawner.ts), revert FIX-2 (server.ts), revert FIX-1 (env-blocklist.ts delete + restore opencode-process.ts). FIX-1 is the most fundamental — reverting it breaks all downstream fixes.

notes: env-blocklist.ts follows the established pattern of packages/core/src/util (utility modules)
sanitizeEnv naming follows the existing sanitizedProcessEnv convention
Import from @opencode-ai/core/util is the standard cross-package pattern
Suffix-based pattern matching follows the same approach as http-recorder's ENV_SECRET_NAMES regex
debug-packet.ts already has SENSITIVE_PATTERNS — extending to value scanning is adding a complementary pass, not restructuring
